meaning: n.拘束,阻碍物,束缚物vt.拘束,阻碍,束缚
examples: The invention discloses a fishing type trammel net.
Pressure to trammel the markets (banning trading in credit-default swaps, setting up a more pliable European rating agency) should be dismissed as an attempt to shoot the messenger.
It shows that America is willing to shield its textile workers from foreign competition even after the mesh of quotas that currently trammel the global textile industry is undone next year.
Liberty Property, Jones Lang LaSalle, Trammel Crow and other large real estate firms have signed up, giving Urban Media access to 900 million square feet of office space and 4.5 million workers.
"So much talk on Twitter of Steve Jobs, but Twitter HQ has been eerily quiet the past few hours save the clicking of keyboards he tailored, " tweeted Mark Trammel, a Twitter designer.
